title: Reuters: one small business used AI as a startup accelerator
friendlyTitle: AI helped a founder start and grow a real business
summary: Reuters profiles Here Now Health, a mental health platform for foster children whose founder used AI tools to learn startup practices, build a business plan, and refine investor pitches. The company launched in 2025, now employs 16 people, and is certified in three states for Medicaid-funded counseling.
friendlySummary: A founder used AI like a startup coach to learn, plan, and pitch her mental health business. The company now serves foster children and has employees.
whyItMatters: The story makes AI’s productivity promise concrete for non-technical founders: lower-cost advice, faster learning, and quicker company formation. It also shows why economists are watching AI not only as a job-risk story, but as a possible entrepreneurship and productivity story.
friendlyWhyItMatters: AI can help people who do not have expensive advisers or business training. It can make starting something new feel less out of reach.
